message.content: ["Build your own flow cell, for use in standardized flow battery and slurry electrode experimental systems.", "Can use electrolyte chamber for half cell measurements or two of the same for full cell measurements."]
message.reasoning_content:
We are asked: "You split a short research text into its distinct FACETS - the separate aspects, conditions or sub-claims it actually contains (the method, its scope/condition, a result, a limitation). Output ONLY a JSON array of 2-4 short strings, each a self-contained facet in the text's own terms - no invention, no overlap. If the text has only one facet, return one element."

Text: "muntasirms/bench-electrochemical-flow-cell-build: v0.2. Build your own flow cell, for use in standardized flow battery and slurry electrode experimental systems. Can use electrolyte chamber for half cell measurements or two of the same for full cell measurements. "

We need to extract distinct facets. Facets are separate aspects, conditions, sub-claims. The text describes a project to build a flow cell. It mentions: "Build your own flow cell, for use in standardized flow battery and slurry electrode experimental systems." That's one aspect: it's about building a flow cell for standardized experimental systems. Then: "Can use electrolyte chamber for half cell measurements or two of the same for full cell measurements." This is about usage flexibility: half-cell or full-cell measurements. So there are two distinct facets: (1) building a flow cell for standardized electrochemical experiments, and (2) its capability for half-cell or full-cell measurements using the same design.

But note the instruction: "self-contained facet in the text's own terms". So we should extract phrases directly from the text, or paraphrase succinctly using the text's language. The text's own terms: "Build your own flow cell", "standardized flow battery and slurry electrode experimental systems", "electrolyte chamber for half cell measurements", "two of the same for full cell measurements". So two facets might be: "Build your own flow cell for standardized flow battery and slurry electrode experimental systems" and "Can use electrolyte chamber for half cell measurements or two of the same for full cell measurements". But the second one could be split into two facets? Half-cell vs full-cell, but the text presents it as one combined capability: using an electrolyte chamber for half-cell, or two for full-cell. That's a single facet about usage flexibility. So two facets: the purpose/build, and the usage modes.

Another possibility: The text also mentions version "v0.2". That could be a facet about the version? Not sure. It says "muntasirms/bench-electrochemical-flow-cell-build: v0.2." This might be a repository name and version. As a facet, it's a bit ambiguous. The instructions say "no invention" so we should stick to what's in the text. The version is part of the text. So maybe a facet: "v0.2" alone? But that's not a full claim. The whole thing could be seen as one facet: "Build your own flow cell, version v0.2, for use in standardized systems, with half-cell and full-cell measurement options." But the task is to split into distinct facets, meaning separate sub-claims. So version is not a claim, it's an identifier. So I'd ignore it.

Thus, I'll output two facets:
1. "Build your own flow cell, for use in standardized flow battery and slurry electrode experimental systems."
2. "Can use electrolyte chamber for half cell measurements or two of the same for full cell measurements."

But check overlap: none. And they are self-contained. The first is a claim about building for standardized systems. The second is a claim about measurement capabilities. So that fits.

The output should be a JSON array. So: ["Build your own flow cell, for use in standardized flow battery and slurry electrode experimental systems.", "Can use electrolyte chamber for half cell measurements or two of the same for full cell measurements."]

But the instruction says "in the text's own terms" so I copied exactly. However, there's a trailing " " which I'll omit. The text has it, but it's an HTML entity. So I'll remove that.

Thus, final answer.
